svg.title: <size:18>Quick Commerce Rider Matching Service - Class Diagram (Annotated for Interview)</size>

g.text: Quick Commerce Rider Matching Service - Class Diagram (Annotated for Interview)
Trace: "Order placed at dark store. System finds nearest available rider, assigns, rider picks up, delivers in 10 min."
"Order is packed at a dark store.
System needs a rider NOW (10 min SLA).
STEP 4 - How to find the right rider? (KEY STEP)
filter by distance, rank by (rating + load).
for speed. Off-peak: use BestFit for quality."
first to accept wins. (Used for Scheduled.)
then optimally pair riders and orders.
"Zone A has 50 orders but only 10 riders.
idle riders from Zone C to Zone A.
ETA to pickup (2 min), ETA to deliver
"Order packed at dark store -> RiderMatchingService gets
available riders from zone-indexed RiderPool -> MatchingStrategy finds
in Pending state -> rider assigned, state moves through lifecycle (
-> customer, store, ETA tracker all notified (
DeliveryRequest: pickup/dropoff location, priority, vehicle type
Rider: location, status, vehicle, rating. RiderPool indexes by zone
Find candidates: nearest, rating-based, least-loaded, composite
Score riders: weighted distance + rating + load + vehicle fit
Assign: auto-assign (peak) vs broadcast (scheduled) vs batch
LoadBalancer: detect zone overload, surge, rebalance riders
Delivery: Pending -> Assigned -> PickedUp -> Delivered / Failed
Customer, dark store, ETA tracker, metrics all notified
